metadata.title:
RAILROAD WORK TO


BEGIN IN SIX WEEKS

BETWEEN 5000 AND 10000 MEN TO BE EMPLOYED
IN CITY LIMITS ON MILWAUKEE, AND


NORTH COAST LINES.

pages.raw_text:
Between 5000 and 10000 men will
be employed by the North Coast
and Milwaukee roads as soon as
construction is begun within the
city limits, and from present indi-
cations this work will be begun
within a month or six weeks. THE
North Coast is the farther ahead
with its plans and will probably be
in the city some little time ahead
of the Milwaukee.


One of the first items of expendi
ture will be $3.50 600 for street i'm
provements, which will include the
opening up, grading and paving of
streets which will be turned over
to the city to take the place of
those which the corporation will
vacate to the railroad.


All that the North Coast is wait
ing for now is an ordinance which
will include the vacation of certain
streets in the site desired for divis-
ion shops. President Strahorn says
that he will so ahead with the work
as soon as this ordinance is passed.
All building obstructing the right of
way of the North Coast have been
disposed of, with the exception of a
very few cases, which will be set.
tied within the next few days.


The Milwaukee, according to Her.
bert Witherspoon, assistant to
James Walsh. local representative
of the road, will not begin work for
several weeks yet within the city
limits. The construction of the rail
road bridge over the Spokane river
and the condemnation of the OBJ
